memoryGovernance.emptyInjectionExplain: No recent injection explain data yet. The backend returns an empty result until a queryable store is wired in.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.noSessionsHint: Create a session to get started

chat.clearConfirm: Clear all messages in this session?

todos.title: Todos
todos.emptyActivePlan: Plan is active; todos will appear here.

skills.title: Skills
skills.confirmDelete: Remove DB override for "{{name}}"? The FS version (if any) will be restored.

wechatConnection.title: WeChat Bot Connection
wechatConnection.subtitle: When a customer messages clawbot in WeChat, the Agent replies inside WeChat. This page only manages connection and recent contact status; it does not show message content or read ordinary private chats sent to your personal WeChat account.
wechatConnection.disabledHint: Official WeChat Bot channel is not enabled. Ask an admin to enable channel.wechatbot.enabled.
wechatConnection.scanTitle: Scan with WeChat to connect the Bot
wechatConnection.scanHint: After scanning, customers must open clawbot in WeChat and send messages there; ordinary private chats sent to the scanned WeChat account do not enter this system.
wechatConnection.noConversationsHint: Ask customers to message clawbot in WeChat; this page shows recent contact status, not message bodies.

scheduledTasks.title: Scheduled Tasks
scheduledTasks.subtitle: Run inspections, reports, and background agent work on cron or interval schedules.
scheduledTasks.desktopOnly: Please use scheduled task management in a desktop browser
scheduledTasks.formSubtitle: Configure the target, schedule, and agent prompt
scheduledTasks.name: Name
scheduledTasks.descriptionPlaceholder: Helps identify this task in the list
scheduledTasks.cronHint: Use standard 5-field cron, for example daily at 09:00: 0 9 * * *
scheduledTasks.intervalHint: Interval is in seconds, for example 3600 runs hourly.
scheduledTasks.promptPlaceholder: Inspect this repository for test and quality risks, then generate a report
scheduledTasks.intervalRequired: Interval seconds must be a positive integer
scheduledTasks.targetConfigInvalid: Target config must be valid JSON
scheduledTasks.rowAria: {{name}}, press Enter to expand runs, E to edit, R to run now, Delete to delete
scheduledTasks.partialWarning: Some run history failed to load. The task list is still usable.
scheduledTasks.emptyDesc: Create a task to let an agent open scheduled sessions or push messages to IM channels.

chatLanding.title: Start a conversation
chatLanding.subtitle: Ask the AI assistant anything, or pick up where you left off

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.desc: Confirm login and connection status, start from the chat home, and learn the input controls.

loginAndConnection.title: 1. Login and connection status
loginAndConnection.desc: Application routes are auth-protected. Header and sidebar footer show WebSocket/local connection status.
loginAndConnection.detail:
## Login entry

If authentication is enabled, the app opens on the login page before entering the chat workspace. Available login methods are configured by admins in Auth Providers.

## Connection status

Both the normal workspace and admin console open a WebSocket connection. The Header and sidebar footer show connection status:

- Connected: the app can receive streaming chat output, approval notifications, replay events, and task state.
- Disconnected: REST requests may still work, but streaming updates, instant approvals, and live replay may be unavailable.

## When disconnected

First confirm the backend is reachable, then refresh the page. If the login token has expired, the client attempts refresh; if refresh fails, it returns to Login.

startConversation.title: 2. Start from the home page
startConversation.desc: The app opens into the chat workspace. Use the home prompt or recent sessions to enter a conversation.
startConversation.detail:
## Open a conversation

The default entry is the chat workspace, not the old dashboard. The home page shows a start area and recent sessions.

Common paths:

1. Type a question on the home page to create a session and jump into chat.
2. Click a recent session to continue existing context.
3. Use the left sidebar to switch between tasks.

## Session naming

After the first message, the frontend uses the first 20 characters as the session name. You can still organize sessions with tags and stars.
startConversation.tip: The normal sidebar is session-first. Agents and Skills live in the admin console.

sendMessage.title: 3. Send messages
sendMessage.desc: Type in the bottom composer, press Enter to send, and use Shift+Enter for new lines.
sendMessage.detail:
## Input and send

- `Enter`: send.
- `Shift+Enter`: add a line break.
- The textarea auto-grows up to about 240px.
- While the agent is responding, the send button becomes a stop button.

## Stop a running task

Click the stop button to send a cancel command for the current session.

## Errors

If sending fails, the chat page shows an error banner that you can dismiss.

modelAndThinking.title: 4. Choose model and deep thinking
modelAndThinking.desc: When multiple models are available, the composer shows a model selector. Deep Thinking is sent with the next message.
modelAndThinking.detail:
## Model selector

The lower-left selector shows models returned by the backend. Pick a model to switch the active runtime model.

## Deep Thinking

When enabled, the next message includes the `deepThinking` option. Use it for complex analysis, planning, and debugging.

## When to leave it off

Simple Q&A, short edits, and quick lookups usually do not need it.

attachments.title: 5. Attach files
attachments.desc: Use the paperclip, drag files into the composer, or paste images. Each file can be up to 25MB; each message can include up to 10 files.
attachments.detail:
## Supported attachments

The composer supports images, audio, selected video files, PDF, Office documents, text, Markdown, JSON, CSV, XML, YAML, and common code files.

## Upload methods

- Click the paperclip.
- Drag files into the composer.
- Paste images from the clipboard.

## Limits

- 25MB per file.
- 10 files per message.
- Unsupported files show an error above the composer.
attachments.tip: Images render inline. Other files appear as attachment chips with name and size.

sessions.title: Session Management
sessions.desc: Create, search, switch, star, tag, clear, and delete sessions from the sidebar.

sidebar.title: 1. Understand the sidebar
sidebar.desc: The left sidebar is a session list with search, create, replay, guide, preferences, and admin links.
sidebar.detail:
## Normal sidebar layout

The sidebar is centered on sessions:

- Hive brand at the top.
- Search, new session, and session list in the middle.
- Session rows expose star, tag, replay, and delete actions on hover.
- Replay, Guide, Preferences, and Admin live at the bottom.

Collapsed mode shows icons and hover labels.

newSession.title: 2. Create sessions
newSession.desc: The new button reuses an empty session if one exists; otherwise it creates a new one.
newSession.detail:
## Quick create

Click the `+` button next to sidebar search.

The frontend first checks for an empty session:

1. If an empty session exists, it opens that session.
2. Otherwise it creates a session named “New Session”.
3. The first message automatically renames it.

## From home

Typing on the home page also creates a session and sends the pending message.

searchOrganize.title: 3. Search, tag, and star
searchOrganize.desc: Sidebar search matches names and tags. Hover a row to star, edit tags, replay, or delete.
searchOrganize.detail:
## Search

Sidebar search filters by:

- Session name
- Session ID
- Tags

## Tags

Hover a session row and click the tag button to edit tags. Tags appear under the session name.

## Stars

Click the star to mark important sessions for easier scanning.

clearDelete.title: 4. Clear and delete
clearDelete.desc: Use Clear in the chat header to remove messages, or delete the entire session from the sidebar.
clearDelete.detail:
## Clear messages

Click “Clear” in the chat header. This removes message history but keeps the session.

## Delete a session

Hover a session row, click delete, and confirm. Deletion removes the session and its messages.

## Running sessions

Delete is disabled while the current session is actively streaming.
clearDelete.tip: Deletion cannot be undone. Prefer Clear when you only want to restart the topic.

messagesAndTools.title: Messages, Tools, and Approvals
messagesAndTools.desc: Understand streaming replies, tool cards, approvals, todo panels, and message actions.

streamingStates.title: 1. Reply states
streamingStates.desc: Agent replies can include thinking, tool calls, streaming text, error cards, token usage, and timing.
streamingStates.detail:
## Common states

A reply can include:

- Thinking: the system is planning or waiting for the model.
- Tool calls: file, search, command, external service, or other tool activity.
- Streaming text: content appears incrementally.
- Error cards: failures are shown in a collapsible card.

## Metadata

Assistant messages may show input/output tokens, duration, and timestamp.

toolCards.title: 2. Tool cards
toolCards.desc: Tool calls render as collapsible cards. Multiple calls show a parallel count.
toolCards.detail:
## Reading tool cards

Tool cards show name, arguments, status, and result summary.

- Running and error states open by default so you can see what is happening or why it failed.
- Successful tools collapse by default to reduce reading noise.
- Previewable HTML can open in Canvas.
- Todo write tool results render as dedicated todo snapshot cards.

## Parallel tools

When a reply has multiple tool calls, the message shows a “Parallel ×N” badge.

approvals.title: 3. Approvals and permissions
approvals.desc: Risky or confirmation-required operations pause for HITL approval before continuing.
approvals.detail:
## When approvals appear

The system may ask for confirmation before side-effecting actions like writing files, running commands, sending externally, or using restricted tools.

## How to respond

Approval cards can support approve, reject, inspect, provide input, or remember a choice. Your response is submitted to the current task.

## Why it exists

Approvals are a safety boundary, not an error.

messageActions.title: 4. Message actions
messageActions.desc: Messages expose copy, preview, feedback, and regeneration actions.
messageActions.detail:
## User messages

User messages support copy. The last user message can show regenerate when available.

## Assistant messages

Assistant messages support:

- Preview: open the full reply in Canvas.
- Copy: copy the full text.
- Helpful / Not helpful: record quality feedback.

## Regenerate

Regenerate rolls back after the last user message and asks the backend to produce a new reply.

todos.title: 5. Todo panel
todos.desc: When the agent creates a structured plan, a todo panel can appear beside or above the chat.
todos.detail:
## When it appears

The todo panel appears when the session has an executable plan, paused state, or resumable snapshot.

## Layout

- Desktop: right workspace.
- Mobile: near the input area.

## Why use it

It tracks long-running task state without forcing you to read the whole transcript.

workspace.title: Workspace and Settings
workspace.desc: Use Canvas, Replay, Preferences, and user-level WeChat connection.

canvasArtifacts.title: 1. Canvas and artifacts
canvasArtifacts.desc: Markdown, HTML, code, and artifacts can open in the right-side Canvas.
canvasArtifacts.detail:
## What Canvas does

Canvas is the chat-side workspace for long content, code, HTML previews, and artifacts.

## How to open it

- Open artifact cards from assistant messages.
- Use Preview on an assistant message.
- Open previewable tool results from tool cards.

## Mobile behavior

On narrow screens, Canvas overlays chat; on desktop, it sits beside chat.

replayGallery.title: 2. Replay gallery
replayGallery.desc: Replay shows execution traces and supports filters for status, quality, dangerous actions, delegation, and more.
replayGallery.detail:
## Entry points

Open Replay from bottom navigation, or hover a session row and click its replay action.

## Gallery

The gallery supports search, filters, and sorting. Filters include success, error, live, quality error, dangerous, delegation, ACP, and context.

## Replay page

The replay page shows timeline, stats, event details, Agent tree, and playback controls.

preferences.title: 3. Preferences
preferences.desc: Preferences are for personal theme, language, and user-level connections.
preferences.detail:
## Theme

Switch between light and dark mode. The setting is stored locally.

## Language

Switch between Chinese and English. Text updates immediately.

## Difference from admin settings

Preferences are personal. Runtime, permissions, MCP, and IM channel configuration live in Admin Settings.

wechat.title: 4. WeChat Bot connection
wechat.desc: Preferences include user-level WeChat Bot connection status and QR flow.
wechat.detail:
## Entry

Open Preferences and find the WeChat Bot connection panel. It shows status, QR code, recent events, and conversations.

## Connection

Scan or refresh from the panel. Contacts must message clawbot in WeChat for messages to enter the system.

## Note

This is an official wechatbot/iLink user-level connection, not personal private-chat scraping.

adminOverview.title: Admin Overview
adminOverview.desc: Understand admin navigation, dashboard, and permission boundaries.

navigation.title: 1. Admin navigation
navigation.desc: Enter Admin from the normal sidebar footer. The admin sidebar contains Agents, Skills, LLM, users, quality, memory, and settings.
navigation.detail:
## Entry

Click Admin in the normal sidebar footer to open `/admin`. Admin uses a separate sidebar from the chat workspace.

## Current admin navigation

- Overview: inspect node status and core resources.
- Agents / Skills / Multi-Agent: inspect local agents, skills, and remote ACP agents.
- Scheduled Tasks: configure cron or interval agent work.
- LLM Models: configure providers, models, and defaults.
- Users / Usage / Auth Providers: manage accounts, quotas, login methods, and costs.
- Prompt Manager: override built-in prompts and run smoke eval before saving.
- Quality Candidates / Quality Workbench / Memory Governance / Auto Optimization: capture failures, run batch evals, govern memory, and approve optimization suggestions.
- Settings: manage runtime, security rules, MCP, external resources, IM channels, and remote agents.

dashboard.title: 2. Admin dashboard
dashboard.desc: The admin home is for operational status and system overview.
dashboard.detail:
## What to check

Use the dashboard to confirm backend health and loaded resources.

## Difference from the user home

The user home starts conversations. The admin home manages the system.

accessModel.title: 3. Access boundaries
accessModel.desc: AdminGuard protects sensitive admin pages. Unauthorized users should not access protected operations.
accessModel.detail:
## Role boundaries

Users, auth, LLM, Skills, system settings, and quality workbench pages generally require admin permissions.

## Auth behavior

Frontend requests include `auth_token`. On 401, the client attempts token refresh; if refresh fails, it returns to login.

## Operational split

Use the user workspace for chat and the admin console for providers, permissions, runtime, and quality operations.

agentsSkills.title: Agents and Skills
agentsSkills.desc: Inspect local agents, skills, remote agents, and external capability sources.

agents.title: 1. Agents page
agents.desc: Agents shows registered agents, capabilities, and tool relationships.
agents.detail:
## Purpose

Use Agents to understand which agents are loaded and where task routing can go.

## Troubleshooting

If a class of work is not being routed correctly, confirm the expected agent is loaded. For remote agents, also check Admin Settings → Integrations.

skills.title: 2. Skills page
skills.desc: Skills lets admins browse skill packages. The admin route is permission-protected.
skills.detail:
## What Skills are

Skills are declarative instruction and knowledge packages, often described by `SKILL.md`. They are not plain chat messages.

## Loading

The backend loads project, user, and built-in skill roots according to server configuration.

## Recommendation

When a repeatable business workflow stabilizes, promote it to a Skill instead of copying long prompts into every session.

remoteAgents.title: 3. Remote Agents
remoteAgents.desc: Configure remote ACP agents from Admin Settings → Integrations.
remoteAgents.detail:
## Location

Open Admin Settings, then Integrations, then Remote Agents.

## Fields

Remote agent configuration includes name, description, transport, command or URL, and capability tags.

## Recommendation

Keep capability tags precise and avoid routing broad work to unclear remote executors.

multiAgent.title: 4. Multi-Agent page
multiAgent.desc: The Multi-Agent page summarizes local agents, remote ACP agents, health, and delegation policy boundaries.
multiAgent.detail:
## What it shows

Open Admin → Multi-Agent to inspect:

- Local registered agents and capability summaries.
- Configured remote ACP agents, transport, command, or URL.
- Remote agent health state.
- Current policy for `spawn_agent`, read tools, and shell/write tools.

## Difference from Settings

Admin Settings → Integrations edits remote agent configuration. Multi-Agent shows global status and delegation boundaries.

## Troubleshooting

If a remote agent does not work, confirm it is enabled, check health and policy state, then inspect the command or remote URL.

llmProviders.title: LLM Management
llmProviders.desc: Configure providers, model records, and the source of the chat model selector.

providers.title: 1. Provider configuration
providers.desc: The LLM page supports creating, editing, and deleting providers.
providers.detail:
## Provider fields

Common fields include name, provider type, service type, Base URL, API key, and enabled status.

## Supported types

The UI includes OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Gemini, Qianfan, Moonshot, Minimax, and Custom provider types.

## Security

API keys are sensitive. Production should manage secrets server-side or through environment-backed config, never committed frontend files.

models.title: 2. Model configuration
models.desc: Models can be attached to providers or stand alone. Chat model options come from backend model config.
models.detail:
## Model records

A model record usually includes display name, actual model ID, provider, temperature, and max tokens.

## Grouping

The LLM page groups models by provider and also shows orphan models.

## Chat relation

The model selector in chat reads available models from the current node.

runtimeSwitch.title: 3. Runtime switching
runtimeSwitch.desc: When a user changes model in chat, the frontend calls the backend model switch endpoint.
runtimeSwitch.detail:
## Behavior

The selected model is sent to the backend and then displayed as active in the composer.

## Troubleshooting

If the selector is empty:

1. Check that enabled models exist.
2. Check that the backend model list returns data.
3. Check that the user is connected to the intended node.

usersAuth.title: Users and Auth
usersAuth.desc: Manage users, auth providers, roles, status, and quotas.

users.title: 1. User management
users.desc: The users page supports search, provider visibility, role/status changes, and quota edits.
users.detail:
## User list

The page shows email, display name, role, status, auth provider, and quota.

## Common operations

- Search users.
- Adjust role.
- Disable or restore accounts.
- Edit quota.

Only trusted admins should have these capabilities.

usage.title: 2. Usage stats
usage.desc: Usage shows total tokens, total cost, per-model cost, and quality-task related cost.
usage.detail:
## What it shows

Usage reads backend accounting records and displays:

- Total tokens.
- Total USD cost.
- Number of models used.
- Per-model token and cost details.
- Quality task, prompt version, failure type, final status, and quality case cost.

## When to use it

When cost climbs unexpectedly, start with model breakdown, then use quality task and prompt version panels to determine whether evals, replays, or a task class caused it.

## Note

If no data appears, cost tracking may be disabled, there may be no LLM calls yet, or the environment may not be writing `usage_records`.

authProviders.title: 3. Auth providers
authProviders.detail:
## Purpose

Auth providers determine how users log in. A broken provider can lock users out, so validate changes in a test environment first.

## Troubleshooting

Check enabled status, callback URL, credentials, and frontend/backend domain alignment.

rolesQuota.title: 4. Roles, status, and quota
rolesQuota.desc: Roles control admin access, status controls account usability, and quota controls resource usage.
rolesQuota.detail:
## Roles

Admin roles can access protected console pages. Normal users should keep only daily-use permissions.

## Status

Disabled users should lose access to protected resources.

## Quota

Quota limits model and system resource usage in shared environments.

runtimeSecurity.title: Runtime and Security
runtimeSecurity.desc: Manage node info, legacy HITL rollback rules, command rules, MCP, external resources, and IM settings.

systemSettings.title: 1. Settings structure
systemSettings.desc: Admin Settings has System, Security, and Integrations tabs.
systemSettings.detail:
## System

Shows node ID, mode, health, version, uptime, and Agent timeout settings.

## Security

Contains legacy HITL rollback rules and command execution rules.

## Integrations

Contains IM channels, MCP servers, external resources, and remote agents.

permissionRules.title: 2. Legacy HITL rollback rules
permissionRules.desc: These rules are only for the HITL execution channel and strict rollback, not the unified tool policy entry point.
permissionRules.detail:
## Rule meaning

Rules usually include tool name, action, and pattern. Policies are allow, ask, and deny.

## Scope

In minimal mode, the unified ToolPolicy decides whether routine tools are callable; these rules cannot downgrade a unified allow into ask or deny. In strict mode, they act as production rollback and tightening rules.

## Recommendation

Do not trust MCP or unknown tools here. Capability grants, route recall, and dangerous-action decisions belong in the unified policy layer. Click Apply to submit runtime config updates to the backend.

execRules.title: 3. Exec rules
execRules.desc: Exec Rules constrain shell commands. Defaults ask before commands like npm install, git push, and rm -rf.
execRules.detail:
## Why

Shell access is high risk. Exec rules force review before dangerous commands.

## Common policies

- Dependency install: ask.
- Git push: ask.
- Recursive delete: ask or deny.

## Maintenance

Keep regexes specific enough to avoid blocking normal work or missing dangerous commands.

integrations.title: 4. Integrations
integrations.desc: Integrations centralize IM channels, MCP, external resources, and remote agents.
integrations.detail:
## IM channels

Configure Feishu, DingTalk, WeCom, and the official WeChat Bot enable switch. Feishu supports webhook / longconn ingress, ack emoji, streaming cards, and throttling.

## MCP Servers

Configure external MCP servers with stdio, sse, or http transport, plus env, headers, timeout, and args.

## External resources

Register external resources that the system can reference.

## Remote Agents

Configure remote agents connected over ACP. User-level WeChat Bot QR connection stays in Preferences.

mcpTools.title: 5. MCP tool catalog and reload
mcpTools.desc: MCP Servers loads a tool catalog. Applying changes updates runtime config and triggers MCP reload.
mcpTools.detail:
## Tool catalog

The MCP Servers panel attempts to read the current MCP tool catalog. When loading succeeds, it shows server tools, resources, and prompt capabilities; when loading fails, the error is visible but configuration remains editable.

## Apply behavior

Click Apply to submit runtime config, call MCP reload, and fetch the tool catalog again.

## Common misconfiguration

In stdio mode, `command=node` should not be paired with `-y`; `-y` belongs to `npx`. The panel shows an inline warning for this case.

operationsQuality.title: Operations and Quality
operationsQuality.desc: Use scheduled tasks, replay, quality candidates, quality workbench, memory governance, and auto optimization.

scheduledTasks.title: 1. Scheduled tasks
scheduledTasks.desc: Scheduled Tasks creates and manages recurring or timed work.
scheduledTasks.detail:
## Purpose

Use scheduled tasks for periodic checks, evaluations, syncs, or stable business workflows.

## Management

The page includes task table, form, and run history drawer. Check recent runs after creating a task.

replay.title: 2. Replay and trace
replay.desc: Replay helps admins inspect decisions, tool calls, quality events, and execution traces.
replay.detail:
## Admin use

Users can understand process with replay; admins can also diagnose failures, dangerous actions, delegation paths, and context issues.

## Deep links

Replay supports `?step=N` links for sharing exact events.

prompts.title: 3. Prompt Manager
prompts.desc: Prompt Manager overrides built-in system, tool, and sub-agent prompts, with smoke eval before save.
prompts.detail:
## Scope

The page groups known prompt keys under system, tools, and subagents. When there is no database override, Hive uses the go:embed default.

## Edit flow

1. Pick a prompt key.
2. Edit content and optional language.
3. Run smoke eval before saving.
4. Save a database override, or delete the override to restore default.

## When it takes effect

Changes affect later conversation turns. The page notes that propagation may take up to about 30 seconds; already-running model calls are not replaced mid-call.

qualityCandidates.title: 4. Quality candidates
qualityCandidates.desc: Quality candidates turn real failures into evaluation assets.
qualityCandidates.detail:
## Sources

Candidates can come from replay quality events or admin quality workflows.

## Purpose

Use them for replay, evaluation, optimization suggestions, and version comparisons.

qualityWorkbench.title: 5. Quality workbench
qualityWorkbench.desc: Quality Workbench groups rules, replay jobs, batch evals, and version diff analysis.
qualityWorkbench.detail:
## When to use

Use it when behavior regresses or a task category fails repeatedly.

## Workflow

1. Select samples from candidates or history.
2. Create replays or batch evals.
3. Review reports and version diffs.
4. Generate and approve optimization suggestions.

memoryAndOptimization.title: 6. Memory governance and optimization
memoryAndOptimization.desc: Memory Governance and Auto Optimization control long-term memory quality and change rollout.
memoryAndOptimization.detail:
## Memory governance

Inspect, filter, explain, and prune memories to prevent stale or low-quality memories from affecting future tasks.

## Auto optimization

Generate suggestions from quality samples or eval diffs, then approve, apply, or roll them back.

## Principle

Review optimization suggestions before production rollout.

channelsApi.title: Channels and API
channelsApi.desc: Configure org-level IM channels and understand HTTP, RPC, and WebSocket boundaries.

imChannels.title: 1. IM channels
imChannels.desc: Org-level IM channels live in Admin Settings → Integrations. User-level WeChat Bot lives in Preferences.
imChannels.detail:
## Org-level channels

Configure Feishu, DingTalk, WeCom, and similar channels in admin integrations. They typically require app credentials, callback URL, token, secret, or encryption key.

## User-level WeChat Bot

WeChat Bot connection belongs in user Preferences and should not be mixed with org-level channel setup.

webhooks.title: 2. Webhooks and routing
webhooks.desc: External IM events enter through webhook or long connection, then route to agent sessions.
webhooks.detail:
## Typical flow

The backend receives IM events, deduplicates, routes, checks policy/rate limits, runs the agent, and replies through the renderer.

## Recommendations

- Use HTTPS in production.
- Make callback URLs reachable from the platform.
- Keep platform tokens and encryption keys aligned.
- Avoid duplicate delivery when switching webhook/long connection mode.

apiWebSocket.title: 3. API and WebSocket
apiWebSocket.desc: The frontend talks to the backend through `/api/v1` REST/RPC endpoints and WebSocket. Vite proxies `/api` to port 8080 in dev.
apiWebSocket.detail:
## Frontend proxy

The local frontend dev server runs on `localhost:3000`; `/api` proxies to `localhost:8080`.

## Endpoint categories

- Sessions and messages.
- Admin APIs for users, auth, LLM, quality, and runtime config.
- RPC for selected configuration and management methods.
- WebSocket for chat streaming, task state, replay live events, and approval notifications.

## Production

The frontend build outputs to `internal/webui/dist` and is embedded in the Go binary.

remoteAgents.title: Remote Agents
remoteAgents.subtitle: Manage remote Agents connected via ACP protocol
remoteAgents.noAgentsHint: Click "Add Agent" below to connect a remote ACP Agent
remoteAgents.name: Name
remoteAgents.descriptionPlaceholder: Agent capability description for Planner reference
remoteAgents.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio transport
remoteAgents.urlRequired: URL is required for http transport

runtimeConfig.title: Runtime Config
runtimeConfig.permissionRulesHint: These rules only apply to the HITL execution channel and strict rollback. In minimal mode, unified ToolPolicy is the primary authorization source; legacy rules cannot downgrade routine allow decisions to ask/deny or trust MCP/unknown tools.
runtimeConfig.execRulesHint: Define regex patterns to allow, ask, or deny command execution. Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins.
runtimeConfig.mcpServersHint: Configure external MCP servers to expose more tools. Changes hot-reload after saving.
runtimeConfig.mcpHeadersHint: one per line, for example Authorization=Bearer ...
runtimeConfig.mcpNoRuntimeTools: No remote MCP tools are currently registered at runtime.
runtimeConfig.mcpToolsLoadFailed: Failed to load MCP tool catalog
runtimeConfig.mcpNeedRestart: MCP changes require saving to file and restart
runtimeConfig.imChannelsHint: Enable DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, and the official WeChat Bot entry per platform. Changes hot-reload immediately.
runtimeConfig.wechatbotHint: This only controls whether the official WeChat Bot entry is open. Users scan from personal settings to connect clawbot in WeChat. Base URL, credential root, and log level are deployment settings.
runtimeConfig.feishuAckEmojiHint: Emoji the bot reacts with on message receipt (Feishu emoji_type, CamelCase). Get (default 👍) / Typing (⌨️) / disabled.
runtimeConfig.feishuIngressModeHint: webhook needs public HTTPS endpoint at /api/v1/channel/feishu/webhook; longconn doesn't need public IP, bot dials ws.open.feishu.cn:443. CEO ruling: webhook XOR longconn, never both in same process.
runtimeConfig.feishuGapFetchEnabledHint: After longconn reconnect, fetch missed messages during disconnection. Default off.
runtimeConfig.feishuWebhookURLHint: Public URL declaring this process serves webhook (optional, just a declaration; the real config is in Feishu console). Non-empty + longconn=true = startup panic.
runtimeConfig.feishuRegionHint: cn = open.feishu.cn (mainland Feishu); intl = open.larksuite.com (overseas Lark). SDK base URL switches accordingly.
runtimeConfig.feishuReloadHint: After saving config, click here to apply new config to running plugin (no restart needed)
runtimeConfig.feishuStreamingHint: When enabled, agent responses patch the same Feishu card incrementally via event stream. When disabled, falls back to legacy one-shot plain-text reply.
runtimeConfig.feishuThrottleHint: Minimum interval between card updates, default 300ms. Too low hits Feishu rate limits; too high increases perceived latency.
runtimeConfig.feishuShowAgentProgressHint: Display intermediate states like "Agent thinking..." in the card. Default off.
runtimeConfig.channelNeedRestart: IM channel changes require saving to file and restart
runtimeConfig.wechatDesc: WeChat Bot enters settings through an official wechatbot user-level connection; legacy protocols have been removed. It only handles messages sent to clawbot.
runtimeConfig.wechatTip1: Legacy personal WeChat protocol settings have been removed
runtimeConfig.wechatTip2: Users can scan in /settings to connect clawbot through official wechatbot
runtimeConfig.wechatTip3: Enterprise IM channels remain configurable on this settings page
runtimeConfig.wechatTip4: No legacy protocol service is required

canvas.noPreview: Preview not available for this type

externalResources.title: External Resources
externalResources.description: Configure connections to databases, monitoring, APIs and other external systems. Agents use these automatically when needed.
